data.title: Investing in aliens? Government websites soon to be launched by Homeland Security
data.description:
Cue the curiosity surrounding extraterrestrial life. The U.S. Department of Homeland Security has registered two new federal domains, including Alien.gov and Aliens.gov, though the websites aren't alive just yet. "Stay tuned!" White House Principal Deputy Press Secretary Anna Kelly declared in a message that included an alien emoji.

Backdrop: Public interest related to unidentified aerial phenomena (UAP) and unidentified flying objects (UFOs) has increased in recent years due to several congressional hearings on alien life. Certain records on the subject were subsequently unclassified, while former President Joe Biden signed an order that required the National Archives and Records Administration to create a registry of government records related to UAPs. Lately, the chatter has intensified, with past and sitting commanders-in-chief weighing in on the matter.

On a podcast in February, former President Barack Obama made some waves by saying aliens were "real, but I haven't seen them, and they're not being kept in Area 51." He later clarified that he was joking, explaining that "statistically, the universe is so vast that the odds are good there’s life out there," but he "saw no evidence during my presidency that extraterrestrials have made contact with us." President Trump subsequently criticized him for giving "classified information," but later said that "based on the tremendous interest shown," he would order the Department of War and other agencies to release government files related to alien and extraterrestrial life.

Capturing investors: The financial world is already moving on alien investment, with Tuttle Capital launching the UFO Disclosure ETF (UFOD). The fund holds companies expected to benefit from government disclosure, confirmation, or investigation of UAP/NHI (Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena/Non-Human Intelligence) technologies, like the reverse engineering of recovered materials, new alloys and compounds, or alternative fuel and energy storage. "The bipartisan nature of the conversation suggests that real disclosure may be imminent," said Tuttle Capital CEO Matthew Tuttle. "We launched UFOD to give investors a disciplined, actively managed way to get ahead of what could be one of the most consequential geopolitical and economic events in human history." The ETF distinguishes itself from funds like the Procure Space ETF (UFO), which focuses on the broader space economy.

data.title: Bowman Consulting enters $146.7M contract amendment with U.S. government agency
data.description:
• Bowman Consulting Group (BWMN) has entered into a $146.7M contract amendment with an agency of the U.S. government, bringing the value of the total contract to $177.7M.
  • The original contract was entered into in December 2025. Bowman's services are expected to be completed over 36 months.

data.title: Rhythm Pharma higher after FDA label expansion for lead drug
data.description:
Rhythm Pharmaceuticals (RYTM) added ~8% in the premarket on Friday after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) approved a label expansion for its lead asset, setmelanotide, to allow its use in patients with acquired hypothalamic obesity, a rare obesity disorder.

The MC4R agonist, already available in the U.S. as Imcivree to treat obesity due to rare genetic disorders such as Bardet-Biedl syndrome, will therefore be indicated for patients aged 4 years and older with acquired HO.

The decision is supported by data from the company’s Phase 3 TRANSCEND trial, which reached the primary endpoint as patients with acquired HO achieved a -15.8% reduction in body mass index ((BMI)) on average over 52 weeks compared to a 2.6% BMI gain for those on placebo.

Rhythm Pharma (RYTM) said Imcivree is the first and only FDA-approved therapy for acquired HO, and it will be immediately available for the targeted patient population.

data.title: Alibaba, Tencent shed $66B as AI monetization concerns hit stocks: report
data.description:
Alibaba Group (BABA) and Tencent (TCEHY) shed about $66B in market value in a day as investors reacted to a lack of clarity on how the companies plan to monetize AI.

Alibaba’s U.S.-listed shares posted their steepest drop since October, while Tencent saw its worst decline in nearly a year on Thursday, reversing a recent rally fueled by optimism around AI agents. Alibaba’s Hong Kong stock was down as much as 7.3% on Friday.

The selloff highlights growing investor concern over rising AI spending, without a clear path to revenue. Alibaba (BABA) has pledged more than $53B in AI investment over several years, outpacing domestic peers, though still a fraction of the roughly $650B U.S. hyperscalers like Meta Platforms (META) and Amazon (AMZN) are expected to spend in 2026.

These investments are ramping up as China’s consumer slowdown pressures margins. Alibaba (BABA) reported a 67% drop in quarterly net income, adding to concerns about profitability. Total revenue rose only 1.7% to RMB 284.84B ($41.3B) and missed the consensus estimate of RMB 289.79B.

The company is now targeting $100B in annual cloud and AI revenue within five years to offset slowing growth in its core e-commerce business. CEO Eddie Wu outlined the goal following the weak results, underscoring the urgency to generate returns from heavy AI spending.

“Investors are not pushing back on AI spending itself, but on the lack of near-term visibility on monetization,” Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Catherine Lim said. “The key inflection will be when companies can show that AI is driving measurable revenue uplift, whether through cloud, advertising, or transaction conversion. Until then, markets will likely stay cautious.”

data.title: Westport Fuel Systems reports cybersecurity breach, delays financial results
data.description:
• Westport Fuel Systems (WPRT) Friday said that it had identified unauthorized access to its network. This incident involved unauthorized access to certain of Westport’s internal IT business applications along with other business and employee information, the company said in a statement.
  • Immediately upon learning of the incident, the company engaged experts, activated "incident response protocol, and took action to contain the incident," it said, adding the investigation is continuing.
  • Given the timing of the incident during the company’s year-end reporting cycle, it is conducting a review of the integrity and accuracy of its financial results and, therefore, expects to file its annual results after the March 31, 2026, disclosure deadline required under securities laws.

data.title: XPeng Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.07 beats by $0.08, revenue of $3.18B misses by $10M
data.description:
• XPeng press release (XPEV): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.07 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $3.18B (+43.9% Y/Y) misses by $10M.
  • Total revenues were RMB22.25 billion (US$3.18 billion) for the fourth quarter of 2025, representing an increase of 38.2% from the same period of 2024, and an increase of 9.2% from the third quarter of 2025.
  • XPENG's physical sales network had a total of 721 stores, covering 255 cities as of December 31, 2025.
  • XPENG self-operated charging station network reached 3,159 stations, including 2,108 XPENG ultra-fast charging stations as of December 31, 2025.
  • Shares -2.8% PM.

data.title: Prestige Consumer Health to acquire Breathe Right for $1.045B
data.description:
• Prestige Consumer Healthcare (PBH) to acquire the Breathe Right brand and other over-the-counter health products from Foundation Consumer Brands for $1.045 billion in cash under a definitive agreement, according to a filing.
  • The acquired portfolio includes Dimetapp and Anbesol with $200 million in revenue and $95 million in EBITDA. The deal is expected to close in H1 fiscal 2027.
  • Prestige plans to finance the acquisition with cash on hand and a new term loan. 
  • Prestige Consumer Healthcare (PBH) rose 1.6% in premarket trading.

data.title: What analysts are saying about Rivian's new partnership with Uber
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN) is being sized up by analysts after the company announced a multiyear partnership in which Uber (UBER) will invest up to $1.25B in Rivian (RIVN) to build a large fleet of all‑electric robotaxis. The deal calls for Uber (UBER) and its fleet partners to buy at least 10K autonomous R2 SUVs starting in 2028, with options that could take the total to as many as 50K vehicles deployed across about 25 cities in the U.S., Canada, and Europe by 2031. The robotaxis will be fully driverless, available exclusively through the Uber app, and are intended to accelerate Uber’s move into autonomous ride‑hailing while providing Rivian (RIVN) with significant volume and funding.

BNP Paribas analyst James Picariello believes the share price for Rivian (RIVN) on Thursday was fully justified, both by the additional liquidity runway and the validation provided by Uber’s $300M vote of confidence in the EV upstart's autonomy capabilities. "We think this further supports RIVN’s decision to develop autonomy in-house while confirming that it expects to reach commercially viable level 4 capabilities by 2028. RIVN previously disclosed that it expects to roll out point-to-point hands-free navigation (similar to TSLA’s FSD Supervised) by the end of the year, but it had not given a timeline for either unsupervised “hands-free” driving or fully autonomous level 4," wrote Picariello. The investment is also seen as a partial backstop in the unlikely event Rivian's (RIVN) $6B Department of Energy loan falls through.

Stifel maintained a Buy rating on Rivian (RIVN) following the Uber (UBER) announcement. Analyst Stephen Gengaro said the new partnership with Uber is a meaningful positive for both its autonomous driving program and the commercial expansion of the R2 platform. Gengaro and his team see Uber's (UBER) commitment to invest up to $1.25B in Rivian (RIVN) and to purchase up to 50K fully autonomous R2 vehicles as a significant validation of the electric vehicle maker's in-house autonomy stack.

Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco called the Uber (UBER) news a positive development for Rivian (RIZVN) towards building traction with the autonomy platform and as an incremental source of capital as the company bridges its path to profitability. "Similar to Rivian's JV with VW, this deal procures funding from a well-capitalized partner investing heavily to develop autonomous driving technology," updated Percoco. The announcement is seen as building on the company's Autonomy & AI Day in December, where it showcased capabilities in data collection with an advanced sensor suite and autonomous stack, including developing its own custom silicon chips.

As for Uber (UBER), Evercore ISI pointed to the rapid cadence of AV-related developments, with five announcements within one week (Zoox–Uber, Wayve/Uber/Nissan, Motional-Hyundai JV, Uber–NVIDIA, and the Waabi investment).

data.title: Euro Area trade deficit widens in January
data.description:
• Euro Area recorded a trade deficit of Є1.9B in January of 2026, compared with - €1.4B in January 2025.
  • Exports of goods to the rest of the world in January were €215.3B, a decrease of 7.6% compared with January 2025.
  • Imports from the rest of the world stood at €217.2B, a fall of 7.3% compared with January last year.
  • ETFs to track: (GF), (EWG), (DAX), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM).

data.title: Amazon developing Alexa-linked phone to deepen customer reach, Reuters reports
data.description:
• In 2014, Amazon (AMZN) launched its Fire Phone to rival Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (SSNLF), but the device - overseen by Jeff Bezos - was scrapped within a year; now, the company is preparing to try again with a new smartphone, Reuters reported on Friday.
  • The latest effort, known internally as “Transformer,” is being developed within its devices and services unit, according to four ​people familiar with the matter. The phone is considered a potential mobile personalization device that can sync with home voice assistant Alexa and serve as a conduit to Amazon customers throughout the day, the report said.
  • The timeline for the project is unclear, as well as the anticipated price ​of the phone, the revenue Amazon (AMZN) hopes to generate, or the financial commitment Amazon has made to the project
  • Amazon spokesperson declined to comment to Reuters.
